quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! They can also hover in mid-air by flapping their wings in a figure-8 pattern up to 80 times per second. These tiny acrobats need to eat every 10-15 minutes and visit up to 2,000 flowers each day to fuel their incredible energy needs.

short_read.title: The Secret Language of Flowers in Victorian Times
short_read.teaser: Flowers weren't just beautiful in the 1800s—they carried hidden messages of love, friendship, and even warning. Learn how a simple bouquet could tell an entire story without a single word spoken.

medium_read.title: The Return of Sea Otters to the Pacific Northwest
medium_read.teaser: Once nearly hunted to extinction, sea otters are making a remarkable comeback along the Pacific Coast. Their return is transforming entire ocean ecosystems in wonderful and unexpected ways.

long_read.title: The Hidden History of Hungarian Embroidery
long_read.teaser: For centuries, Hungarian women have preserved stories, symbols, and regional identities through intricate needlework. This beautiful tradition connects generations and reveals the soul of village life across the Carpathian Basin.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer in Olympic National Park
port_angeles.description: June is a wonderful time to spot Columbian black-tailed deer with their new fawns in the meadows around Port Angeles. These gentle creatures are most active during early morning and evening hours, and Hurricane Ridge offers some of the best viewing opportunities. The does carefully hide their spotted fawns in tall grass while they forage nearby.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart can beat up to ___ times per minute during flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! That's an incredibly fast heartbeat—about 20 beats every second!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute during flight—that's amazingly fast!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: A hummingbird's tiny heart actually beats about 1,200 times per minute during flight—that's 20 times per second! It's hard to imagine such speed.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To fuel this incredible heart rate, hummingbirds have the highest metabolism of all animals. They must consume about half their body weight in sugar daily, which is why they visit so many flowers.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Beautiful! Pillangó is indeed butterfly—such a lovely, dancing word for such a lovely, dancing creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó'—doesn't it sound light and fluttery? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language unrelated to most European languages. It belongs to the Finno-Ugric family and is known for its beautiful, poetic words and complex but logical structure.

puzzle_3.question: What is the Strait of Juan de Fuca named after?
options: A Greek explorer sailing for Spain
puzzle_3.correct_response: Wonderful! Ioannis Phokas, known as Juan de Fuca, was a Greek navigator who sailed for Spain in the 1590s. He claimed to have found this passage between the Pacific Ocean and what is now Washington State.
puzzle_3.wrong_response: The strait is named after Juan de Fuca (Ioannis Phokas), a Greek navigator who sailed for Spain in the 1590s. He reported discovering this important waterway that you can see right from Port Angeles!
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: While historians debate whether Juan de Fuca actually sailed through the strait himself, the waterway was officially named in his honor in the late 1700s. It's about 96 miles long and connects the Pacific Ocean to Puget Sound.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
